profile.name: VISHAL VERMA
profile.title: Cloud & Systems Admin
bio: Recent Master of Information Technology graduate with a specialisation in Internetworking from the University of Technology Sydney.
Bringing 9+ years of IT operations experience across cloud infrastructure, systems administration, and enterprise solutions. Proficient in Azure, AWS, and VMware.

services.title: CLOUD INFRASTRUCTURE
services.description: Expertise in designing scalable cloud architectures using Microsoft Azure, AWS (EC2, EKS, S3, RDS), and VMware vSphere/vCenter for 99.98% uptime.

services.title: SYSTEMS ADMINISTRATION
services.description: Proficient in Windows/Linux Administration, Nginx, Backup & Disaster Recovery, and Cloud Migration with a focus on high-availability enterprise solutions.

services.title: NETWORKING & SECURITY
services.description: Advanced routing principles, multilayer switched networks, TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, firewalls, and mitigation of electromagnetic interference (EMI).

services.title: IT OPERATIONS & LEADERSHIP
services.description: Managed IT-enabled workflows, risk assessments, and digital documentation for 110+ personnel while maintaining a zero-incident safety record.

projects.title: Multi-Vendor Network Automation & Observability Engine
projects.intro: A scalable, multi-threaded network discovery and auditing engine.

richDetails.content: Managing enterprise-grade, multi-vendor network environments (Cisco IOS-XE, NX-OS, IOS-XR) relies heavily on manual CLI access. This method is slow, prone to human error, and fails to provide a unified "source of truth" for the network state. Furthermore, standard configuration management does not offer real-time visibility into operational trends.

I engineered a high-performance, multi-threaded automation framework that bridges the gap between raw CLI data and actionable network intelligence. By leveraging Infrastructure as Code (IaC) principles, I transformed manual audit workflows into a standardized, repeatable automated process.

content: **Automation Core:** Developed in Python 3.8+, utilizing Netmiko for abstracted, secure SSH interactions across heterogeneous device models.

**Decoupled Inventory:** Implemented a YAML-based inventory system, separating device-specific variables (IPs, credentials, platform modes) from the execution logic to ensure modularity and scalability.
**Concurrency:** Utilized Python's `concurrent.futures` to enable multi-threaded execution, allowing for parallel data collection across dozens of network nodes.
**Error Handling:** Architected robust exception management using context managers, ensuring graceful handling of connection timeouts, authentication failures, and unreachable devices.
**Data Processing:** Processed unstructured CLI output into structured data formats.
**Visualization:** Integrated with Grafana (via a time-series database backend like InfluxDB/Prometheus) to visualize operational status, interface utilization trends, and compliance health.

rows: Core logic and automation script execution
Parallel device polling for high-speed audits
Real-time observability dashboard for operational metrics

content: **80% Efficiency Gain:** Reduced the time required to perform a comprehensive network-wide operational state audit from hours to minutes.
**Standardization:** Created a unified interface for operational data retrieval, eliminating discrepancies between vendor-specific command outputs.
**Observability:** Enabled proactive monitoring by feeding audit data into Grafana, allowing for real-time visualization of network health rather than relying on reactive troubleshooting.
**Scalability:** The framework easily adapts to new hardware; adding a device or a vendor platform simply requires updating the YAML inventory, not altering the core engine.

projects.title: Meshmap
projects.intro: An off-grid P2P tactical mapping & messaging system.
details: MeshMap is a decentralized, infrastructure-free peer-to-peer (P2P) mapping and messaging system designed for deep-wilderness, disaster-struck, and tactically compromised environments.

Instead of relying on internet connectivity or establishing power-heavy Bluetooth pairings, the application leverages raw Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) advertisements to broadcast location telemetry and chat messages asynchronously into the ambient airwaves.

Under the hood, it features a native Android layer written in Kotlin and Java that uses runtime Reflection to bypass standard SDK constraints, forcing the hardware radio to scan across all physical layers including the long-range Coded PHY.
To fit complex GPS coordinates into legacy 31-byte BLE payloads, MeshMap uses a custom Base36 offset-shifting compression algorithm that reduces the spatial data payload by 47%.
projects.conclusion: The resulting application enables critical off-grid coordination across different devices by concurrently broadcasting over legacy 1M PHY and Extended Coded PHY channels, bridging the gap between legacy consumer hardware and cutting-edge Bluetooth 5.0 capabilities.

projects.title: HubHop
projects.intro: A real-time Sydney transit planner & telemetry dashboard.
details: HubHop is a high-performance, real-time transit routing application built for the Transport for NSW (TfNSW) network. It moves beyond standard scheduled routing by tapping directly into the absolute source of truth: the raw, binary GTFS-Realtime Protocol Buffer stream.
This allows HubHop to track trains and transit services across Sydney with sub-minute precision, catching micro-adjustments and delays instantly as they happen on the tracks.

Operating on a Hybrid Orchestration Pipeline, the frontend pings the backend Trip Planner API for scheduled routes, while simultaneously decoding the live GTFS-Realtime array to dynamically fuse delay telemetry into the UI.
projects.conclusion: The end result is a lightning-fast, edge-rendered transit dashboard capable of tracking Sydney's complex train network without breaking a sweat. It demonstrates the power of tapping directly into raw binary telemetry streams rather than relying on bloated JSON proxies, achieving sub-second UI updates across thousands of concurrent connections.

projects.title: Portfolio
projects.intro: Personal portfolio website built with modern web technologies.
details: This portfolio was designed from the ground up to showcase my skills and projects with a sleek, minimalist aesthetic. It relies on Tailwind CSS for a custom utility-first design system and fully responsive layouts.

Under the hood, it uses Vite for blazing fast hot module replacement during development and an highly optimized production build, achieving perfect Lighthouse scores for performance and accessibility.

projects.title: TrueNode Astro
projects.intro: An AI-powered astrology, tarot, and celestial tracking workspace.
details: TrueNode Astro is a premium, state-of-the-art Web application that integrates live astronomical calculations, biorhythm waves, tarot spreads, and deep conversational AI guidance. Built on Next.js, Supabase, and Google Gemini, it provides a visually stunning, highly interactive portal for self-reflection and celestial alignment.

The Cosmic Dashboard displays your profile summary, active retrogrades, a real-time list of planetary positions, and upcoming celestial events. It accurately tracks planetary transits mapped against your birth natal chart using the Astronomy-Engine and maps them into correct Astrological Houses.

It features a digital Tarot Oracle that analyzes card spreads via Gemini AI, a Biorhythm Tracker mapping 30-day physical and emotional cycles, and a Cosmic Chat that cross-references your exact birth chart and live transits to provide hyper-personalized AI guidance.
projects.conclusion: With comprehensive features like the Synergy Engine calculating romantic synastry between two profiles and Row-Level Security ensuring absolute data privacy, TrueNode Astro functions as a highly secure, feature-rich cosmic oracle.

projects.title: SmartV Hybrid Cloud
projects.intro: Designed and deployed a highly available hybrid cloud architecture ensuring 99.99% uptime.
details: SmartV Hybrid Cloud was a major infrastructure initiative that bridged on-premises VMware workloads with Microsoft Azure. The goal was to provide seamless failover, disaster recovery, and scalable compute for enterprise applications.

Using Terraform, the entire infrastructure was defined as code, allowing for rapid provisioning and consistent environments. The architecture achieved a 99.99% SLA by utilizing Azure Availability Zones and Traffic Manager.

projects.title: Peermindful AI Platform
projects.intro: Architected a scalable backend for an AI-driven mental health platform.
details: Peermindful is a platform that uses AI to connect users with peers and provide mental health resources. The backend was architected to handle sensitive data securely and scale dynamically with user load.

Deployed on AWS Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS), the system uses microservices written in Python to interface with large language models. HIPAA compliance was a major focus during the architecture design phase.

projects.title: Edumap.io Location Platform
projects.intro: Migrated legacy educational mapping services to a containerised microservices architecture.
details: Edumap.io provides geospatial data and routing for educational institutions. The legacy monolithic architecture was struggling to keep up with increasing traffic and feature requests.

I led the migration to a containerized microservices architecture using Docker. By breaking down the monolith and implementing an Nginx API Gateway, we reduced deployment times by 70% and significantly improved system reliability.

testimonials.quote: Demonstrated expertise in designing scalable cloud architectures, optimising system performance, and leading technical projects.
